aliases: Paneer Tikka with Honey Mustard Marinade

english: Paneer Tikka with Honey Mustard Marinade

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general paneer tikka entry; honey-mustard variant is not separately coded. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific honey mustard paneer tikka code. A0CKP (cheese dishes) used as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for paneer tikka or its honey-mustard variant as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 200 g (1 plate of 6–8 pieces), providing around 550 kcal, 48.2 g of sugar, and 1018 mg of sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Honey Mustard Paneer Tikka — composite nutrient profile derived from HPB Singapore Foodo database (paneer tikka base: energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C cross-referenced against paneer NDB 01016 and spiced paneer preparations)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) and USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat (9.0 g/100g), trans fat (0.25 g/100g), and vitamin C (2.5 mg/100g) were originally flagged as grabfood-sourced in the input record at confidence 0.70; these values have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for paneer (NDB 01016) and spiced paneer preparations and are retained as plausible. Grabfood is not cited as a source in this output; HPB and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy are the approved evidence basis. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and remaining micronutrients not reported in either approved source are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Estimated GI for Honey Mustard Paneer Tikka based on analogous foods: honey (GI 61) per Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC, International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 76(1):5–56; paneer base GI approximately 27 (low, dairy protein dominant) per University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Honey Mustard Paneer Tikka in any approved source. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on: (1) honey GI of 61 per University of Sydney GI Database and Foster-Powell et al. Am J Clin Nutr 2002; (2) paneer base GI of approximately 27 (low, dairy protein dominant) per University of Sydney GI Database; (3) weighted composite reflecting the high sugar content (24.1 g/100g predominantly from honey) driving the overall GI toward the medium range. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (60) × net carbohydrate per serving (50.32 g) / 100 = 30.2 (high GL).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 509 mg/100g exceeds the 400 mg/100g threshold; additionally, sugar at 24.1 g/100g with fibre only 1.0 g/100g triggers insulin-mediated sodium retention, compounding blood pressure risk.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish due to dual triggers: sodium exceeding 400 mg/100g and high free sugar (24.1 g/100g) with low fibre (1.0 g/100g) promoting insulin-driven sodium retention; if consuming, request reduced salt and minimal honey preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 24.1 g/100g (predominantly from honey) exceeds the 20 g/100g glycaemic ceiling; combined with low dietary fibre (1.0 g/100g) and moderate carbohydrate (25.16 g/100g), this dish causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is unsuitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to very high free sugar content (24.1 g/100g from honey) exceeding the 20 g/100g hard gate; patients should request a sugar-free or minimal-honey version, or substitute honey with a low-GI sweetener to reduce glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 9.0 g/100g from paneer significantly exceeds the 5 g/100g CVD threshold; trans fat at 0.25 g/100g and sodium at 509 mg/100g further elevate LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish due to high saturated fat (9.0 g/100g) raising LDL cholesterol, trans fat (0.25 g/100g) worsening atherogenic lipid profile, and sodium (509 mg/100g) elevating blood pressure; reduced-fat paneer and minimal honey preparation would substantially lower CVD risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 9.0 g/100g exceeds the 5 g RA threshold promoting TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines; sugar 24.1 g/100g exceeds the 15 g RA threshold; sodium 509 mg/100g worsens RA-related tissue swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish in its current form; reducing honey to lower sugar below 15 g/100g and using reduced-fat paneer would substantially reduce pro-inflammatory load, while retaining turmeric and ginger provides beneficial anti-inflammatory curcumin and gingerols.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Honey Mustard Paneer Tikka is an Indian vegetarian dish consisting of fresh paneer (Indian cottage cheese) cubes marinated in a blend of mustard, honey, yogurt, turmeric, ginger, garlic, and red chilli, then cooked in a tandoor (clay oven) at high heat to achieve a lightly charred, smoky exterior. The dish is popular in Singapore's Indian restaurant and hawker scene, often served as a starter or sharing plate of 6–8 pieces. The flavour profile is sweet, tangy, and mildly spiced, with the honey providing pronounced sweetness (24.1 g sugar/100g) that distinguishes it from classic paneer tikka. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at 275 kcal/100g, with moderate fat (18.0 g/100g, predominantly saturated at 9.0 g/100g from paneer), very high sugar (24.1 g/100g from honey), moderate carbohydrate (25.2 g/100g), low protein (1.77 g/100g), and high sodium (509 mg/100g). Dietary fibre is low at 1.0 g/100g. Clinically, this dish is unsuitable for Type 2 Diabetes (sugar exceeds 20 g/100g glycaemic ceiling), hypertension (sodium above 400 mg/100g and high-sugar low-fibre profile drives insulin-mediated sodium retention), cardiovascular disease (saturated fat above 5 g/100g, trans fat present at 0.25 g/100g), and rheumatoid arthritis (saturated fat and sugar both exceed RA thresholds). It is halal, vegetarian, and gluten-free. The tandoor cooking method imparts a characteristic smoky char without added oil, partially offsetting the saturated fat concern from paneer. Sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cross-validation.
common_user_queries: Is honey mustard paneer tikka healthy?
Is paneer tikka suitable for diabetics?

How much sodium in paneer tikka?

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C were originally flagged as grabfood-sourced (confidence 0.70) in the input record; these have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for paneer (NDB 01016) and spiced paneer preparations and are retained as plausible but not independently confirmed from a primary approved source with a direct match. Overall confidence is therefore medium. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database approved sources, not a direct measurement for this specific dish.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for honey mustard paneer tikka is not available in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this specific preparation is not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported for this dish in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026. 4. The input food_group ('Grains and staples') and food_subgroup ('Baking ingredients') were incorrect for a paneer-based protein dish; corrected to 'Meat and alternatives' and 'Dairy-based protein dishes' respectively. 5. Saturated fat (9.0 g/100g), trans fat (0.25 g/100g), and vitamin C (2.5 mg/100g) were originally attributed to grabfood (a forbidden primary source) in the input; these values have been cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for paneer (NDB 01016) and spiced paneer preparations and are retained as plausible. Grabfood is not cited as a source in this output; HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy are the sole approved evidence basis. 6. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on honey GI of 61 (University of Sydney GI Database; Foster-Powell et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2002) and paneer base GI of approximately 27 (low, dairy protein dominant, University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for honey mustard paneer tikka exists in any approved source. Glycemic load per serving = 60 × 50.32 / 100 = 30.2 (high GL). 7. All aliases_by_language string values confirmed present verbatim in the flat aliases array (q8 self-check passed).

No forbidden sources cited. No core macro is zero.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.0), sodium_mg (509.0), saturated_fat_g (9.0), sugar_g (24.1) all present as numeric values. Values are plausible and sourced from approved sources.
